title: Multi-platform images: copy from platform
body:
**Description**

I want just to extract current platform chroot from image (uses another platform), but buildah can't find it based on current platform.

```docker
FROM x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu_prefix AS prefix
FROM scratch
COPY --from=prefix /usr/i686-unknown-linux-gnu/ /
```

```bash
buildah bud --platform="linux/i686" .
```

```
error creating build container: short-name "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu_prefix" did not resolve to an alias and no unqualified-search registries are defined in ""
```

This issue can be fixed by using `--platform="linux/amd64"` instead of `--platform="linux/i686"`, but I am not agree with using another platform type.

**How to reproduce**

`gentoo/stage3` comes with 32 bit `ld` and `libc`, trying to use it:

```docker
FROM docker.io/gentoo/stage3 as data
FROM docker.io/i386/busybox
COPY --from=data /lib/*-2.32.so /tmp/
RUN /tmp/ld-2.32.so /tmp/libc-2.32.so
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```bash
buildah bud --platform="linux/amd64" .
```

Everything is fine, but image platform is wrong.

```bash
buildah bud --platform="linux/i386" .
```

```
error creating build container: Error choosing an image from manifest list docker://gentoo/stage3:latest: no image found in manifest list for architecture i386, variant "", OS linux
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
buildah version 1.20.1 (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.2-dev)
```

This issue is reproducible for any OS.

I think this issue is related to #1590, it looks like `FROM --platform` will be the best solution.

comments.body:
Currently there is no docker.io/gentoo/stage3:latest  "linux/i386" image on docker.io which is why the command is failing?  

You would like to allow the second command to specify the platform, so something like

```
FROM docker.io/gentoo/stage3 as data
FROM --platform="linux/i386" docker.io/i386/busybox
COPY --from=data /lib/*-2.32.so /tmp/
RUN /tmp/ld-2.32.so /tmp/libc-2.32.so
```

I am not sure this worked, but it did not blow up.

```
$ buildah bud --layers=false -f /tmp/Dockerfile /tmp
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/gentoo/stage3 AS data
--> b17ee8a69d1
STEP 2: FROM docker.io/i386/busybox
Trying to pull docker.io/i386/busybox:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d3376651214e done  
Copying config 30bf2a4039 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 3: COPY --from=data /lib/*-2.32.so /tmp/
STEP 4: RUN /tmp/ld-2.32.so /tmp/libc-2.32.so
GNU C Library (Gentoo 2.32-r7 p6) stable release version 2.32.
Copyright (C) 2020 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
This is free software; see the source for copying conditions.
There is NO warranty; not even for MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A
PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
Compiled by GNU CC version 10.2.0.
libc ABIs: UNIQUE IFUNC ABSOLUTE
For bug reporting instructions, please see:
<https://bugs.gentoo.org/>.
STEP 5: COMMIT
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d36cb77d8923 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob b798bb3fdec2 done  
Copying config 554ab34793 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 554ab347935
554ab34793512f35be9024fa7ce49a6b1390c2f6a1b65b433185329bc4f2d5da
```

I think `from --platform as ...` will be better.

```docker
FROM --platform="linux/amd64" docker.io/gentoo/stage3 as data
FROM docker.io/i386/busybox
```

```bash
buildah bud --platform="linux/i386" --layers=false -f /tmp/Dockerfile /tmp
```

It will mean that our image uses `i386` platform only, `amd64` image used just for some data extraction. For now this command fails.

I don't think this makes much sense, from the command line you are overriding the first FROM command all other commands can not be overridden, I guess you could play with ARGS if you wanted other from lines to be overridden.

We have to pass `platform` somehow because `FROM scratch` for example has no platform by default.

```
FROM scratch
buildah bud --platform="linux/i386"
```

It will provide image with platform `architecture: "i386"` instead of default `architecture: "amd64"` as we may want.

But when we are trying to add another image as data source, it fails, because it tries to use first defined architecture:

```
FROM scratch
FROM docker.io/gentoo/stage3 as data
buildah bud --platform="linux/i386"
```

> no image found in manifest list for architecture i386

So I think we need `platform` for `FROM`.

The only one possible dirty hack for today is to use `buildah from` + `buildah mount` + `bud ---volume dir` for data.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

So what happens when I do

FROM scratch --platform="linux/arm64"
buildah bud --platform="linux/i386"

The override of scratch seems to work now
```
$ buildah bud --arch arm64 -f - . <<< "FROM scratch"
STEP 1: FROM scratch
STEP 2: COMMIT
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 5f70bf18a086 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 46f9f6c0e2 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 46f9f6c0e2c
46f9f6c0e2c370dd0f28c1e5d7511dd7e27b82a2aa53f5977cf5566abb28f4ee
buildah (block) $ buildah inspect 46f9f6c0e2c370dd0f28c1e5d7511dd7e27b82a2aa53f5977cf5566abb28f4ee | grep -i arch
    "Config": "{\"created\":\"2021-06-26T10:19:16.962424972Z\",\"architecture\":\"arm64\",\"os\":\"linux\",\"config\":{\"Env\":[\"PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin\"],\"Labels\":{\"io.buildah.version\":\"1.21.1\"}},\"rootfs\":{\"type\":\"layers\",\"diff_ids\":[\"sha256:5f70bf18a086007016e948b04aed3b82103a36bea41755b6cddfaf10ace3c6ef\"]},\"history\":[{\"created\":\"2021-06-26T10:19:16.96297096Z\",\"created_by\":\"/bin/sh\"}]}",
        "architecture": "arm64",
        "architecture": "arm64",
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
